card_description: Jesus/Christ is a historical figure referenced in Philip K. Dick's Exegesis. Appears in 2 Exegesis passages. Context: "After a business letter regarding a lost $500 check, Dick pivots to a theological breakthrough: the Kingdom of God is located in the "Future," and he reached it by "running out of time" in March 1974."

linked_segments.summary: After a business letter regarding a lost $500 check, Dick pivots to a theological breakthrough: the Kingdom of God is located in the "Future," and he reached it by "running out of time" in March 1974.
linked_segments.title: 1975-09-15_SECTION_012_01.txt

linked_segments.summary: Dick develops the "Scramble Generator" model: the "Black Iron Prison" (BIP) is a machine (Yahweh/Demiurge) that scrambles our perceptions. He argues that Christ/OCCP is a "telos seed" (explosive) smug
linked_segments.title: 1976-09-15_Dorothy_78.txt
